name: Valerie Foushee
subject: A simple way to make a big difference
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Foushee for Congress
body:
There has never been a more meaningful time to give to a food bank.

If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/9_07fM1dnEmqdVR1wv8kcQ2?unsubscribedata=JxE6NwlhaeHElBCYwzbgbyuyjt3r%2BpsylmLSNr6iBzs8MIPMtc%2B5VUMfyY6pMgjDBRmeCIREWu4abtSohAH88RcY6rzaTdyY5XxWsynKh7FEN6sdEziUHq8vyN1kvEcDFEoPnZtddmrr1AIRAFaMTNVP3%2BdrqQ6qF%2BediGBUNK0CBLr2WkwlZzKVwUo1Snm%2FPRn5VdQrIvz1GHOOoYJS0OIJe548%2BBlHWXV6tKffsAe5cFMfHCd6i1QqbNw6K0z3Hnnum6vJxF5c%2FqxDEAsbv6RHJ%2BF8Ca6pRQGQ%2BLNK2J7cBs%2BUZKMkarK%2FnZHvrokhIxQ9%2FBRuo8liIsknfmgzL8s%2FQWbg7ck8659TI0HIrVxacirDlvkBTnugDMYRrAKQpjNoqPW1OfoZukvM2klMamGW0T66LL5F4pI1Lg5tkfY%3D)
---
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119650075/583787298/-1546188038?source=SBD_VF_EM_ENG_112025_FOODBANKS_180DO_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZTJmZmRjMC0zMmM2LWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wxqQqMqYRQnnbQXvcnVdRJh403VMCi57uLyaMM6xVIY=)
---
Peter,

This time of year, I’m always reminded of the power and importance of coming together to support one another. I’m especially thinking of the many families affected by the recent government shutdown. Between missed paychecks, the rising cost of groceries, and cuts to SNAP benefits, it has been more difficult for some households to put a Thanksgiving meal on the table. But no one should have to choose between paying a bill and sharing a holiday with the people they love. So as the holiday season approaches, there has never been a more meaningful time to give to a food bank in your community. The added pressures of this season can weigh heavily on families already stretching every dollar, and even a small act of generosity can bring tremendous relief and hope to someone in need. For those in North Carolina, you can find ways to support local food pantries here: **[https://foodbankcenc.org/](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119650083/583787305/-87671395?source=SBD_VF_EM_ENG_112025_FOODBANKS_180DO_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZTJmZmRjMC0zMmM2LWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wxqQqMqYRQnnbQXvcnVdRJh403VMCi57uLyaMM6xVIY=&emci=dbe2b150-50c5-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=8e2ffdc0-32c6-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=799895)** If you’re outside of North Carolina, you can locate your nearest food bank by entering your ZIP code here: **[Feeding America.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119650090/583787312/1380771237?source=SBD_VF_EM_ENG_112025_FOODBANKS_180DO_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZTJmZmRjMC0zMmM2LWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wxqQqMqYRQnnbQXvcnVdRJh403VMCi57uLyaMM6xVIY=&emci=dbe2b150-50c5-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=8e2ffdc0-32c6-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=799895)** And if a financial donation isn’t possible right now, consider donating your time. Even a few hours of volunteering can make a world of difference for families who rely on these services. Our strength as a community is measured by how we show up for one another. Thank you for taking the time to read this, and for considering a contribution, whether it’s time, resources, or support, in whatever way you’re able. With gratitude,
Valerie Foushee

* * *
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119650112/583787320/-1370552099?source=SBD_VF_EM_ENG_112025_FOODBANKS_180DO_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZTJmZmRjMC0zMmM2LWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wxqQqMqYRQnnbQXvcnVdRJh403VMCi57uLyaMM6xVIY=&emci=dbe2b150-50c5-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=8e2ffdc0-32c6-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=799895) | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119650116/583787343/174071783?source=SBD_VF_EM_ENG_112025_FOODBANKS_180DO_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZTJmZmRjMC0zMmM2LWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wxqQqMqYRQnnbQXvcnVdRJh403VMCi57uLyaMM6xVIY=&emci=dbe2b150-50c5-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=8e2ffdc0-32c6-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=799895) | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119650120/583787347/230632730?source=SBD_VF_EM_ENG_112025_FOODBANKS_180DO_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZTJmZmRjMC0zMmM2LWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wxqQqMqYRQnnbQXvcnVdRJh403VMCi57uLyaMM6xVIY=&emci=dbe2b150-50c5-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=8e2ffdc0-32c6-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=799895)

clean_body: There has never been a more meaningful time to give to a food bank. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | = Peter, This time of year, I’m always reminded of the power and importance of coming together to support one another. I’m especially thinking of the many families affected by the recent government shutdown. Between missed paychecks, the rising cost of groceries, and cuts to SNAP benefits, it has been more difficult for some households to put a Thanksgiving meal on the table. But no one should have to choose between paying a bill and sharing a holiday with the people they love. So as the holiday season approaches, there has never been a more meaningful time to give to a food bank in your community. The added pressures of this season can weigh heavily on families already stretching every dollar, and even a small act of generosity can bring tremendous relief and hope to someone in need. For those in North Carolina, you can find ways to support local food pantries here: If you’re outside of North Carolina, you can locate your nearest food bank by entering your ZIP code here: And if a financial donation isn’t possible right now, consider donating your time. Even a few hours of volunteering can make a world of difference for families who rely on these services. Our strength as a community is measured by how we show up for one another. Thank you for taking the time to read this, and for considering a contribution, whether it’s time, resources, or support, in whatever way you’re able. With gratitude, Valerie Foushee * | | | ---|---|--- = Valerie is fighting to protect access to health care, working families, and to reform our criminal justice system. But she needs your help to continue to do this great work in Congress. Your donation right now will help protect Democrat Valerie Foushee by giving us a jumpstart on the next cycle. | = = Paid for by Foushee for Congress Valerie Foushee for Congress PO Box 16446 Chapel Hill, NC 27516 United States If you need to you can unsubscribe, You can also =.

body:
My father and I couldn’t do this without patriots like you. Open this note of gratitude. |
Fellow American, it's Don Jr. I wanted to take a moment to **express my heartfelt gratitude** for **your unwavering support**. My father and I know that this fight **isn't possible without patriots like you** who are willing to stand up for what's right.

We're honored to have you on our team, **Fellow American.**

**[As a small token of our appreciation, I'd like to share a special message with you. body:
Every GOP voter should verify their profile ASAP
Dear Fellow American,
I am reaching out with a **critical** request.
For decades, the radical left has allowed open borders that flooded their states with illegal immigrants. Now, they want to turn them into voters - but not on our watch.

subject: What I’d tell you over coffee

body:
I will never take your sacrifice for granted.
[](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=0c0bf905fb7940e4a1dd40fdf9e270c0&_e=P0ZeuqWxwQYCkSp1_QExV4l7Ai5sJ1ds8xJXnPRCaPMUofIZHDPJY-21aPS3IaVWEvWEDdbGljrIeXRpW5PzUg4KydJwvr1ISi7-DbEwj-yCvZoklSlOdeu42APSBda51Mvmf6Gr5Sjh1zvfDsNsx72mIDNuqIYMzB79icep7DaTVILGxUU4KUJTAu9ASNhiblREZN7ysu5vNEFjSajJ2GkWHqzl52175mkAAgrxyp1Z2ARnfNkL2urSIlKx5uTT0CH5limLp9dMriv56giFNjnELXUB4Sk4pBkvWmcR0CYCczO_4G30_Hnc5KVk03olXB3UbTFGM9Id-w_Q2fp7Y9mIvX16mWJsq5K8fxhiWkIjxMq9gpOpnDfdSAstygkGY2krOa1gWeroX6sxmAhNsSaJxlQ2V-6a68GUBBC9af2_rwGpHoKPxq198UOVnxy3QmW6QY7h6lfvpqeKGllL2skOpq3L9xJRh1o8Yx6arFi_ab8OGx1zUWCrp9UuIDXELvrLSuQzICMDzXTE7vIrH56vyNFt_UtuZdVg87MdE8OrU_2fvwbl0YAfvrKNh4-_29S5eq4sEJyXCRHJelarRjgqaa7uGN8UestMsKPOadGxnb1rw62qwQigL4mWqFaGDBIgz9uOTC5-1NyUNR7TADk2KLFDhpjic6AOIj_pEK38SCZK8xc2XCcQH3vzZvmuL3TfHuBs4G0b9H8jCA0cxfruKYzdaJTEUP2L8Q-kg_sK0QtuhEwR9EDTxsTA2RQ2_JqF6EGB-ixerX3VBSG0sl911tXXxnz5oxhM3PqRIjHVeqJM5fdv3qweJuF3r8hxyL4kkZg6d-o08aVQwqkvds3rjfEdvhcmwYGawXL6_PpkESJcS0J5B1g-HVYslDfycI4tmE_9J0UyafFvJcJQWREnQu4H3QPLkLL_Lou26nM%3D)Politics today is full of noise - phony deadlines, scare tactics, and cheap gimmicks.
But I want to cut through the noise for a moment.
[I’d like to tell you a quick story.](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=0c0bf905fb7940e4a1dd40fdf9e270c0&_e=P0ZeuqWxwQYCkSp1_QExV4l7Ai5sJ1ds8xJXnPRCaPMUofIZHDPJY-21aPS3IaVWEvWEDdbGljrIeXRpW5PzUg4KydJwvr1ISi7-DbEwj-yCvZoklSlOdeu42APSBda51Mvmf6Gr5Sjh1zvfDsNsx72mIDNuqIYMzB79icep7DaTVILGxUU4KUJTAu9ASNhiblREZN7ysu5vNEFjSajJ2GkWHqzl52175mkAAgrxyp1Z2ARnfNkL2urSIlKx5uTT0CH5limLp9dMriv56giFNjnELXUB4Sk4pBkvWmcR0CYCczO_4G30_Hnc5KVk03olXB3UbTFGM9Id-w_Q2fp7Y9mIvX16mWJsq5K8fxhiWkIH3j5mDTku1-We6TCAZfh-ciAmiKHeZZSM25G4p2OkFHUu2v1LTYQy5eK2_ufNUZWfT3beP65pQa2Rz7W4OdZ3hNdYQUVvWK6zclbg7ZqzxlVVgnqwxtoPeHyIhQ7bpCjLjIsgxHfSi0HcA6meL_3N6nCbeYo5ezJvwSmrWI9MuGbEYOxwadZ672bSKEkI3Z2N_fen3fqubcZKGhdtvJVYH1CPhgJgCTPWi2CpqOX0dy9O-6JOcMrqgHgAmNHb2Z1m70NIgGoy2ePt47LqlNq3pLHpNQvt1Eacxyj64WdKQ8RWoRpjb9pCjSv59xGArC2b6zcA3J5PY2uwMEMsrW_r1zfiJ84wMyVta78C6pgFqPoMDUVCoAILH5VgYIBmXMEMY5-sjy9HuYx6ZB87ST1HNIL5yAG-p0omusdHoG3GS11KlNxGDkFtfvVoaTOO6I1RDmNxQ9lTsC74x4n_wvYnSsZYMwIZI0VJxasoLaYiG3-TKhjOg64Ey1krU8jDZeNnGSrTfXe_b5CNxmoaXDqSDb6Cox3fY-kmrGn2ub6Bc9yxoD-1vFmP1LFqUmX9tHc%3D)
I grew up right here in Ohio. My parents worked hard to give me a chance at the American Dream, and I know what sacrifice looks like. Every opportunity I've had was built on the back of that sacrifice.[](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=0c0bf905fb7940e4a1dd40fdf9e270c0&_e=P0ZeuqWxwQYCkSp1_QExV4l7Ai5sJ1ds8xJXnPRCaPMUofIZHDPJY-21aPS3IaVWEvWEDdbGljrIeXRpW5PzUg4KydJwvr1ISi7-DbEwj-yCvZoklSlOdeu42APSBda51Mvmf6Gr5Sjh1zvfDsNsx72mIDNuqIYMzB79icep7DaTVILGxUU4KUJTAu9ASNhiblREZN7ysu5vNEFjSajJ2GkWHqzl52175mkAAgrxyp1Z2ARnfNkL2urSIlKx5uTT0CH5limLp9dMriv56giFNjnELXUB4Sk4pBkvWmcR0CYCczO_4G30_Hnc5KVk03olXB3UbTFGM9Id-w_Q2fp7Y9mIvX16mWJsq5K8fxhiWkKXxWJcsr-PwBGnsGtQgZ0qdCFG3AQ_7cVExt4cOHLU1aXUHa_eXbrdFdTbnGcghq7v_mzMxB-QkFtR6npg24UABf_eowP5lm95VgFor1bBoQVC5ACSqOrG8G0xXXi2ZwVoRWRev1WSn0L3Opmeaumr2pej0H5Ggcguk7R9ddGZ_nGN3w_1pWCcBb_J4hrcZVPmWCQQ5Fm5LfuMz_PIcH7Y22bGCxFaGK_EDb1yq-s8narEDW-Y9n6H8L9e2YtZXxEomVWgVHjSf0Og9tM74gWfuvvxeVcUD83vRQgLaqa8MPaOxeKTgIXscJ-kyJsSWGJtmn9ONe0GridSVCnvWlDS2Bs8pKFUHRkagklZ91SR7W6LWonN4SWZzdiW9QUQnkvYPuX9VEsJAo7NUYa3FOUKYTPnpshcSU8jLiUkoF_zjcYUf6bgNNc6P8LSter1YqxMusudYKv6nXfKSTRoAxndqI_weujgajYTWx0avMwnu9YUWp_giv5fUOnjUY_J5lclEOBZ7oEWbJfPTNLctCJdmDkyRC-HDF8GEll2IwOYqD7KzEthZeMhwx8EJmak87U%3D)That's why I never forget what it means when someone like you chips in a few dollars. It isn't "just money." **
**It 's trust. It's belief. It's a vote of confidence in the movement we're building in Ohio and beyond. **
**So whether you 've already donated or you're considering it today, let me say this clearly: **
**[THANK YOU, Peter!](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=0c0bf905fb7940e4a1dd40fdf9e270c0&_e=P0ZeuqWxwQYCkSp1_QExV4l7Ai5sJ1ds8xJXnPRCaPMUofIZHDPJY-21aPS3IaVWEvWEDdbGljrIeXRpW5PzUg4KydJwvr1ISi7-DbEwj-yCvZoklSlOdeu42APSBda51Mvmf6Gr5Sjh1zvfDsNsx72mIDNuqIYMzB79icep7DaTVILGxUU4KUJTAu9ASNhiblREZN7ysu5vNEFjSajJ2GkWHqzl52175mkAAgrxyp1Z2ARnfNkL2urSIlKx5uTT0CH5limLp9dMriv56giFNjnELXUB4Sk4pBkvWmcR0CYCczO_4G30_Hnc5KVk03olXB3UbTFGM9Id-w_Q2fp7Y9mIvX16mWJsq5K8fxhiWkJGTixBG7gpAfIBXU_PE2BdZHJduMz2eydbHfyzYKGyfoyKzABDXIut3YFiB8MGD7JIsmQJm_OPSVsh4opfnQbgxmOOET4sw8hLzK1Ii8G4JXNND191T1mJaWj2U37a47TvB1SVVN21BuMgFgwg1ViByh3aONGKkHA2Rgm5GkmZxmS6YKJ1bVwM4HRsxfUx_OLdyWAMlDI21bzmGHHBq1zb1WHdfEtQOh4AxKFyqdcKV52clf5fn2EEkJ_3p56rdLU17s4pxFlrUO6dZLfqPZJMY63YhycQWSqNASI119czXklkLvlthG085mSSY-GY6o23MialRxpjHMl4nmDKTjz7_7ZBcOXcgRyz2OBabMaJy7T8WXkW226IJZb8CFLUXrbBuEHr266fFHkBIPiuDUoDDn1mwnOQyWMJn382UyN9tOVzqbz7zOvMiJjAmsMhDq0DJILrzSCx2jL3f0XAfSc4tbB8YayuDHn1rwRV8txTbsPknatO-mwJjDN5HzdVQy8yZqZRPeMN4p6rcVx_z6kp6ffdmCwRodPsVL2oebIwp8FTEQC0vaoVaHz1bGFYaQo%3D)**
**We 're in an extremely tough fight. And this isn't just any race - it's shaping up to be one of the most important in the country.**
**I threw my hat in the ring now because we have a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to save our country. I mean that. We were[ ONE ELECTION AWAY](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=0c0bf905fb7940e4a1dd40fdf9e270c0&_e=P0ZeuqWxwQYCkSp1_QExV4l7Ai5sJ1ds8xJXnPRCaPMUofIZHDPJY-21aPS3IaVWEvWEDdbGljrIeXRpW5PzUg4KydJwvr1ISi7-DbEwj-yCvZoklSlOdeu42APSBda51Mvmf6Gr5Sjh1zvfDsNsx72mIDNuqIYMzB79icep7DaTVILGxUU4KUJTAu9ASNhiblREZN7ysu5vNEFjSajJ2GkWHqzl52175mkAAgrxyp1Z2ARnfNkL2urSIlKx5uTT0CH5limLp9dMriv56giFNjnELXUB4Sk4pBkvWmcR0CYCczO_4G30_Hnc5KVk03olXB3UbTFGM9Id-w_Q2fp7Y9mIvX16mWJsq5K8fxhiWkJlkHbgr9xCuOh5-v8C0a1o4bLaaZVRg9ErgXQXlPGFbQ6NNlQm_xX38_bol0YUY-bFWhTnT1wObDVqKlUjk0v819TQPr-PHyoeOIQbCjpwfYiG6BXmBNM6KSbmThiiM6f_gA6G6u6h_cS6GNBn1z3KOytrnMIMPWxsH_Y0x_ML7Erl4CrmLCKB-4sXoRkktKTy-D-pUQ26UUiJuqz_i9cVsjWjPWwHSFynplW6EMFW7LPbo7jP8xdC3z94VvY7cQE99HobS4MEVvh9t0JLpEABQv4B3jH6KprtanWuZNKmQYoF5WBawy9A4hmI9F6Ay6tuP1bvoQqOjLzlyDFl1td5bpQFWseVo6-SbXfGbFR5sOKagi1OE-xljcpC7r3t0HALmBY0trpNEykYHBpl0KCyXwuv2OBtd3U0zS5FN6Do5WWQCToGAjR1on_GA9RcsL0U5AdR6_ir5ymjj1Iao-OR2Xk8kjGSiBUgKgBbw_Wyt2MUtqMMwjRjZqZSJS2f-stopRDIlqHgfRN9ZQnHOxxwwYIXSTuod4MekVkO6iE27yAfrU-IfylIbu8hDDuRqHE%3D) from a country you and I wouldn't recognize anymore.**
**President Trump saved us - with the help of folks like you - from that dire outcome. With that said, he needs loyal partners at the state level to help him succeed. **
**That 's why this race means so much. **
**So if you 've given before (even just once, even just a buck) know this: you've already made a difference, and I will never take your sacrifice for granted. **

clean_body: I will never take your sacrifice for granted. today is full of noise - phony deadlines, scare tactics, and cheap gimmicks. But I want to cut through the noise for a moment. I grew up right here in Ohio. My parents worked hard to give me a chance at the American Dream, and I know what sacrifice looks like. Every opportunity I've had was built on the back of that sacrifice. why I never forget what it means when someone like you chips in a few dollars. It isn't "just money." It 's trust. It's belief. It's a vote of confidence in the movement we're building in Ohio and beyond. So whether you 've already donated or you're considering it today, let me say this clearly: We 're in an extremely tough fight. And this isn't just any race - it's shaping up to be one of the most important in the country. I threw my hat in the ring now because we have a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to save our country. I mean that. We were from a country you and I wouldn't recognize anymore. President Trump saved us - with the help of folks like you - from that dire outcome. With that said, he needs loyal partners at the state level to help him succeed. That 's why this race means so much. So if you 've given before (even just once, even just a buck) know this: you've already made a difference, and I will never take your sacrifice for granted. It’s George Conway. I’ve spent _years_ calling out Donald Trump, the Republican Party, and their enablers! **And if there’s one man who embodies political cowardice more than anyone else, it’s _Lindsey Graham_.**

Here’s the truth: Lindsey Graham is as spineless as they come.

We’d lose a true champion for working families just to be replaced by yet another MAGA rubber-stamp for Trump’s extreme agenda.
**And worse — if we lose Colorado’s seat, we’ll lose ANY chance we had at taking back our Senate Democratic majority.**
Without the Senate, we’ll continue to see no action on bringing down health care costs. We’ll get more legislation that benefits the wealthiest among us at the expense of working people. **And there’s not a chance in hell Republicans in Congress take a stand against the chaos and dysfunction coming out of the Trump administration.**

# U.S. Jobs boom shocks Wall Street amid Shutdown chaos!
In a **[surprise jolt to jittery markets,](https://substack.com/redirect/2869cfae-3acc-4ce2-8ed1-d76e5664c8cf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** the U.S. economy added a robust 119,000 jobs in September--far exceeding forecasts of 50,000--before Democrats launched a crippling 44-day government shutdown which stalled data flows.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2869cfae-3acc-4ce2-8ed1-d76e5664c8cf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Payrolls Surge Unexpectedly** : Nonfarm employment beat Dow Jones estimates by more than double, signaling pre-shutdown resilience despite August's revised loss of 4,000 jobs and July's downward tweak to 72,000.
* **Wages Cool Moderately** : Average hourly earnings rose 0.2% monthly--below the 0.3% forecast--and 3.8% annually, easing inflation pressures that could sway Federal Reserve's December rate pause.
* **Sectors Show Divergence** : Health care and leisure sectors gained 43,000 and 37,000 jobs respectively, while transportation shed 25,000 amid federal hiring cuts totaling 97,000 for the year.
* **Household Data Optimistic** : Employment swelled by 251,000, boosting participation to 62.4%--a five-month high--with full-time roles up 673,000.

# **Nvidia shrugs off "Bubble" fears as Q3 results ignite a 5% surge in the stock**
Nvidia **[shattered Wall Street expectations Wednesday](https://substack.com/redirect/f5343eea-d850-47db-ac5a-e707fa7a8448?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** with blowout third-quarter earnings and a $65 billion sales forecast, as CEO Jensen Huang dismissed AI bubble fears amid surging global demand--except in restricted China, where sales cratered to just $50 million.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/f5343eea-d850-47db-ac5a-e707fa7a8448?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Massive Backlog Fuels Growth** : Nvidia's AI chip orders hit $500 billion for 2025-2026, excluding fresh deals with Anthropic and Saudi Arabia, signaling explosive infrastructure investments.
* **Huang Rejects Bubble Fears** : CEO emphasized three booming AI drivers--GPU software, novel apps, and power-hungry "agentic AI"--predicting deeper appreciation for the tech surge beyond mere spending hype.
* **China Sales Vaporize Amid Tensions** : Geopolitical curbs and competition slashed H20 chip revenue to insignificant levels, despite new U.S. export licenses sharing 15% with the government.
* **Analysts Cheer Reprieve Rally** : Wall Street hailed the results as a "hand-holding" win, projecting $400 billion in free cash flow over nine quarters while doubting a quick China recovery.

**NVIDIA 'S AI FURY IGNITES 5% SURGE!**
Nvidia shares **[rocketed 5% in after-hours trading](https://substack.com/redirect/54d90710-6a11-4cee-8c58-f5ace1723d2f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** Wednesday after the chip giant reported blowout third-quarter results, with revenue soaring 62% to $57 billion and a sunnier forecast quashing AI bubble fears.
* **Blowout Revenue Triumphs Forecasts** : Third-quarter sales hit $57.01 billion, smashing analyst predictions and surging 62% from last year, fueled by unrelenting AI chip hunger.
* **Huang Shatters Bubble Doubts** : CEO Jensen Huang dismissed AI hype as illusory, insisting Nvidia's view reveals "something very different"--sustained hyperscaler and enterprise demand.
* **Robust Q4 Outlook Boosts Confidence** : Company guidance suggests stronger-than-expected fourth-quarter sales, spotlighting needs from OpenAI, Anthropic, and sovereign AI initiatives.
* **Margins and Peers Ride Wave** : Gross margins exceeded estimates amid supply hurdles; the rally caused a wider rally across global AI stocks like AMD, Broadcom, and Samsung Electronics.

* * *
* * *
# US approves AI chip sales to Saudi Arabian tech giants
The U.S. Commerce Department **[approved exports of up to 35,000](https://substack.com/redirect/90c9e45b-1df0-431b-a144-d4367fd87e8a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** advanced Nvidia AI chips to Saudi Arabia's HUMAIN and the UAE's G42 on Wednesday, easing prior restrictions after Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman's Washington visit and a $1 trillion investment pledge to bolster American tech leadership.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/90c9e45b-1df0-431b-a144-d4367fd87e8a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Export Reversal Signals Shift** : The move overturns Biden-era curbs aimed at blocking tech leaks to China, prioritizing U.S. economic ties despite security risks.
* **Rigorous Safeguards Imposed** : The terms of the deal impose mandates, strict reporting, and oversight by Commerce's Bureau of Industry and Security to monitor chip deployment and prevent unauthorized distribution.
* **Partnerships Fuel Infrastructure Boom** : HUMAIN has signed deals with Adobe, Qualcomm, AMD, Cisco, Groq, Luma and xAI for a massive 500-megawatt Saudi data center.
* **Global Hub Ambitions Soar** : Saudi Arabia aims to become the world's third-largest AI power by 2026, compressing two decades of progress into one year.

* * *
* * *
# Democrat Congresswoman charged with stealing Covid cash
Federal prosecutors **[charged Democratic Rep. Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick](https://substack.com/redirect/6db926f9-ba55-4f30-add5-3a72257f63ec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** with stealing $5 million in FEMA COVID-19 funds, laundering them through family accounts and funneling portions illegally to her 2021 congressional campaign via a straw donor scheme, the Justice Department announced Tuesday.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/6db926f9-ba55-4f30-add5-3a72257f63ec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **FEMA Overpayment Fuels Fraud** : A $5 million error in a family health-care firm's COVID vaccination contract provided the illicit funds, obtained in July 2021 and the money was quickly laundered to obscure origins.
* **Siblings Spearhead Straw Scheme** : Cherfilus-McCormick and her brother Edwin allegedly routed money to friends and relatives as "donors" to her campaign, violating finance laws and implicating Nadege Leblanc in the plot.
* **Tax Fraud Hides Lavish Spending** : She conspired with preparer David Spencer to falsify returns, claiming personal and political costs as business deductions while inflating charitable gifts to slash liabilities.
* **Ethics Probe Looms Larger** : Ongoing House investigations, sparked by a 2024 referral, eyes further violations like steering public funds to for-profits, with the congresswoman facing up to 53 years' prison if convicted.

* * *
* * *
# The Starbucks strike is the holiday gift no one wants
In a bold escalation timed for peak holiday frenzy, Starbucks Workers United **[expanded its strike to 95 stores across 65 U.S. cities](https://substack.com/redirect/01e5fd2d-bc99-4e80-beaf-bc6b48c9a735?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** Thursday, involving 2,000 baristas demanding better wages, hours and fair practices amid stalled talks since last year.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5e58f6b4-d398-43f5-9e8e-28a1df0c0657?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Strike Scope Expands Dramatically** : The Union added over two dozen cities and stores since the November 13 start, targeting 95 locations nationwide with open-ended action by 2,000 workers, including pickets at a key Pennsylvania distribution center.
* **Holiday Sales Face Threats** : The action coincides with record-breaking Red Cup Day traffic up 44.5%, potentially jeopardizing Starbucks' seasonal sales rebound under CEO Brian Niccol despite no observed disruptions yet.
* **Core Demands Remain Unmet** : Workers seek proposals on wages, scheduling and resolution of hundreds of unfair labor charges, after rejecting the company's April economic package and entering mediation earlier this year.
* **Company Counters Minimal Impact** : Starbucks insists 99% of 17,000 U.S. stores are staying open, touting $30 hourly pay averages, low turnover and readiness to bargain, while blaming union for negotiation breakdowns.

clean_body: U.S. economy added a robust 119,000 jobs in September--far exceeding forecasts of 50,000 | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? U.S. economy added a robust 119,000 jobs in September--far exceeding forecasts of 50,000 | | | Nov 20 | | | | | | | | | | | | Hello Capitalists, Here is everything you should be following today: Jobs report shocks Wall Street Nvidia earnings pump stock price US approves AI chip sale to Saudi Arabia Congresswoman busted for Covid Cash fraud, faces 53 years Starbucks Strike expands as holiday season approaches Today 's markets + assets: 🔴 DOW: 45881.81 (⬇️ 0.56%) 🔴 S&P: 6587.20 (⬇️ 0.80%) 🔴 NASDAQ: 22307.92 (⬇️ 1.08%) ⚠ ️✅CBOE VIX Volatility Index: 27.96 (⬆️ 18.17%) 🔴 Gold: $4058 (⬇️ 0.6%) 🔴 Silver: $50.20 (⬇️ 1.27%) 🔴 Bitcoin: $86,345 (⬇️ 6.80%) The Capitalist is a reader-supported publication Reject Corporate Left-Wing Journalism U.S. Jobs boom shocks Wall Street amid Shutdown chaos! In a the U.S. economy added a robust 119,000 jobs in September--far exceeding forecasts of 50,000--before Democrats launched a crippling 44-day government shutdown which stalled data flows. | | ---|---|--- Payrolls Surge Unexpectedly : Nonfarm employment beat Dow Jones estimates by more than double, signaling pre-shutdown resilience despite August's revised loss of 4,000 jobs and July's downward tweak to 72,000. Wages Cool Moderately : Average hourly earnings rose 0.2% monthly--below the 0.3% forecast--and 3.8% annually, easing inflation pressures that could sway Federal Reserve's December rate pause. Sectors Show Divergence : Health care and leisure sectors gained 43,000 and 37,000 jobs respectively, while transportation shed 25,000 amid federal hiring cuts totaling 97,000 for the year. Household Data Optimistic : Employment swelled by 251,000, boosting participation to 62.4%--a five-month high--with full-time roles up 673,000. Your Complimentary 90-Day AMAC Membership America is at a crossroads -- and your voice matters. That's why we're inviting you to try | | ---|---|--- When you activate your complimentary membership, you'll receive immediate access to: Exclusive Savings on travel, insurance, healthcare, and more Trusted Resources on Medicare, retirement, and Social Security A Voice in Washington through AMAC Action, our powerful grassroots advocacy arm Community & Fun with the AMAC Magazine, book club, sweepstakes, and more No cost. No credit card needed. No obligation. Just 90 days of everything AMAC has to offer -- so you can experience the difference for yourself. [[Activate Your Free 90-Day Membership]]() Join today and stand with millions of like-minded Americans fighting to protect faith, family, and freedom. P.S. You'll also start receiving AMAC Magazine , the trusted publication conservatives rely on for news and insight you won't find anywhere else. Nvidia shrugs off "Bubble" fears as Q3 results ignite a 5% surge in the stock Nvidia with blowout third-quarter earnings and a $65 billion sales forecast, as CEO Jensen Huang dismissed AI bubble fears amid surging global demand--except in restricted China, where sales cratered to just $50 million. | | ---|---|--- Massive Backlog Fuels Growth : Nvidia's AI chip orders hit $500 billion for 2025-2026, excluding fresh deals with Anthropic and Saudi Arabia, signaling explosive infrastructure investments. Huang Rejects Bubble Fears : CEO emphasized three booming AI drivers--GPU software, novel apps, and power-hungry "agentic AI"--predicting deeper appreciation for the tech surge beyond mere spending hype. China Sales Vaporize Amid Tensions : Geopolitical curbs and competition slashed H20 chip revenue to insignificant levels, despite new U.S. export licenses sharing 15% with the government. Analysts Cheer Reprieve Rally : Wall Street hailed the results as a "hand-holding" win, projecting $400 billion in free cash flow over nine quarters while doubting a quick China recovery. NVIDIA 'S AI FURY IGNITES 5% SURGE! Nvidia shares Wednesday after the chip giant reported blowout third-quarter results, with revenue soaring 62% to $57 billion and a sunnier forecast quashing AI bubble fears. Blowout Revenue Triumphs Forecasts : Third-quarter sales hit $57.01 billion, smashing analyst predictions and surging 62% from last year, fueled by unrelenting AI chip hunger. Huang Shatters Bubble Doubts : CEO Jensen Huang dismissed AI hype as illusory, insisting Nvidia's view reveals "something very different"--sustained hyperscaler and enterprise demand. Robust Q4 Outlook Boosts Confidence : Company guidance suggests stronger-than-expected fourth-quarter sales, spotlighting needs from OpenAI, Anthropic, and sovereign AI initiatives. Margins and Peers Ride Wave : Gross margins exceeded estimates amid supply hurdles; the rally caused a wider rally across global AI stocks like AMD, Broadcom, and Samsung Electronics. US approves AI chip sales to Saudi Arabian tech giants The U.S. Commerce Department advanced Nvidia AI chips to Saudi Arabia's HUMAIN and the UAE's G42 on Wednesday, easing prior restrictions after Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman's Washington visit and a $1 trillion investment pledge to bolster American tech leadership. | | ---|---|--- Export Reversal Signals Shift : The move overturns Biden-era curbs aimed at blocking tech leaks to China, prioritizing U.S. economic ties despite security risks. Rigorous Safeguards Imposed : The terms of the deal impose mandates, strict reporting, and oversight by Commerce's Bureau of Industry and Security to monitor chip deployment and prevent unauthorized distribution. Partnerships Fuel Infrastructure Boom : HUMAIN has signed deals with Adobe, Qualcomm, AMD, Cisco, Groq, Luma and xAI for a massive 500-megawatt Saudi data center. Global Hub Ambitions Soar : Saudi Arabia aims to become the world's third-largest AI power by 2026, compressing two decades of progress into one year. Democrat Congresswoman charged with stealing Covid cash Federal prosecutors with stealing $5 million in FEMA COVID-19 funds, laundering them through family accounts and funneling portions illegally to her 2021 congressional campaign via a straw donor scheme, the Justice Department announced Tuesday. | | ---|---|--- FEMA Overpayment Fuels Fraud : A $5 million error in a family health-care firm's COVID vaccination contract provided the illicit funds, obtained in July 2021 and the money was quickly laundered to obscure origins. Siblings Spearhead Straw Scheme : Cherfilus-McCormick and her brother Edwin allegedly routed money to friends and relatives as "donors" to her campaign, violating finance laws and implicating Nadege Leblanc in the plot. Tax Fraud Hides Lavish Spending : She conspired with preparer David Spencer to falsify returns, claiming personal and political costs as business deductions while inflating charitable gifts to slash liabilities. Ethics Probe Looms Larger : Ongoing House investigations, sparked by a 2024 referral, eyes further violations like steering public funds to for-profits, with the congresswoman facing up to 53 years' prison if convicted. The Starbucks strike is the holiday gift no one wants In a bold escalation timed for peak holiday frenzy, Starbucks Workers United Thursday, involving 2,000 baristas demanding better wages, hours and fair practices amid stalled talks since last year. | | ---|---|--- Strike Scope Expands Dramatically : The Union added over two dozen cities and stores since the November 13 start, targeting 95 locations nationwide with open-ended action by 2,000 workers, including pickets at a key Pennsylvania distribution center. Holiday Sales Face Threats : The action coincides with record-breaking Red Cup Day traffic up 44.5%, potentially jeopardizing Starbucks' seasonal sales rebound under CEO Brian Niccol despite no observed disruptions yet. Core Demands Remain Unmet : Workers seek proposals on wages, scheduling and resolution of hundreds of unfair labor charges, after rejecting the company's April economic package and entering mediation earlier this year. Company Counters Minimal Impact : Starbucks insists 99% of 17,000 U.S. stores are staying open, touting $30 hourly pay averages, low turnover and readiness to bargain, while blaming union for negotiation breakdowns. name: April Christenson
subject: Re: An OLCV donor wants to meet you, Peter!
body:
Hello again,
I wanted to make sure you saw my last email and got a chance to read longtime OLCV supporter Pete's story. Our donors are what keeps OLCV alive and serving as Oregon's political arm for the environment.
Right now we are raising the resources that will determine what can accomplish next year. From beginning our endorsement process in January, to the February short session, to the election in November - there are so many things we can achieve for our climate and environment. But we need your support in order to get there. We can't do this without you, so will you make a generous gift of $ 50 to help us win this fight? [https://secure.ngpvan.com/t28-fgcXRUmjEwNoP96SgQ2?am=20&contactdata=pZHxQXbdScqlk6TlSMzwITtDBcuMUn/ikoYwX8PDNzxly/U/scE30CxVhn2Q6QEcP8qvV443Xk+Tl3+iPh6GN2Azm6bL9CNL922vsoan36km4W/rJltyvFRVqgTthP0N]
Give now [https://secure.ngpvan.com/t28-fgcXRUmjEwNoP96SgQ2?am=50&contactdata=pZHxQXbdScqlk6TlSMzwITtDBcuMUn/ikoYwX8PDNzxly/U/scE30CxVhn2Q6QEcP8qvV443Xk+Tl3+iPh6GN2Azm6bL9CNL922vsoan36km4W/rJltyvFRVqgTthP0N]
With gratitude,
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/AV/AVLCV/1/70877/images/April%27s%20Signature.png]
April Christenson (she/her)
OLCV Development Director
P.S.: We are in the big push for end of year fundraising. If you would no longer like to receive emails related to end of year fundraising, please fill out this form here to opt-out [https://secure.everyaction.com/c2_U_ki-NEyU8Gdkttquug2] but still remain on our general email list!
https://secure.ngpvan.com/t28-fgcXRUmjEwNoP96SgQ2?am=50&contactdata=pZHxQXbdScqlk6TlSMzwITtDBcuMUn/ikoYwX8PDNzxly/U/scE30CxVhn2Q6QEcP8qvV443Xk+Tl3+iPh6GN2Azm6bL9CNL922vsoan36km4W/rJltyvFRVqgTthP0N [https://secure.ngpvan.com/t28-fgcXRUmjEwNoP96SgQ2?am=50&contactdata=pZHxQXbdScqlk6TlSMzwITtDBcuMUn/ikoYwX8PDNzxly/U/scE30CxVhn2Q6QEcP8qvV443Xk+Tl3+iPh6GN2Azm6bL9CNL922vsoan36km4W/rJltyvFRVqgTthP0N]
Hi Peter,
This year is almost over, and recently I’ve been taking the time to look back at all that OLCV has accomplished. Although we’ve had an uphill battle with the Trump Administration ever since he took office, there is one thing that gives me comfort and hope: our dedicated OLCV members . None of our work is possible without our members like you, Peter. We can’t do it without you, so our important work begins with your generous gift of $50. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/t28-fgcXRUmjEwNoP96SgQ2]
Give now [https://secure.ngpvan.com/t28-fgcXRUmjEwNoP96SgQ2?am=50&contactdata=pZHxQXbdScqlk6TlSMzwITtDBcuMUn/ikoYwX8PDNzxly/U/scE30CxVhn2Q6QEcP8qvV443Xk+Tl3+iPh6GN2Azm6bL9CNL922vsoan36km4W/rJltyvFRVqgTthP0N]
To show how important our members are to our work, I’d like to share this donor’s story with you. Meet Pete Sandrock. Pete has been a member of OLCV for a long time. He lives in Portland, Oregon. This is Pete’s story:
"I care deeply about Oregon, its people, and its natural environment. I was born here, the great grandson of Sherman County homesteaders. My mother taught me to love and protect nature. I still have the bird book she gave me in 1955. For high school graduation, she gave me Rachel Carson’s Silent Spring. Mom and dad were active New Deal Democrats. I remember meeting Edith Green when my parents hosted a campaign coffee for her in our Rose City Park neighborhood home. As a 13-year-old I knocked on doors, collecting one dollar donations for JFK, later meeting him at [https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/474C684466775A64434166713668646D39324B525A6F6C715962353265346C4F5377714E4D69503138504D79506A6B446F455746357733306D61686558443951/editor_images/Council%20Crest%20Streetcar%202-22-1950%20copy.jpg]
Cleveland High School (I just wish I knew what I did with his autograph!). In 1964 I joined some of my Jesuit High School classmates to picket George Wallace at the old Portland airport. My sign read, “If Hell is Free, Why Can’t Alabama Be?” JFK’s famous admonition, “ask not what your country can do for you – ask what you can do for your country,” led me to 44 years of government service, from the Navy to elected office to appointed management positions.
The sum of those experiences: love of place, love of nature, and appreciation for effective government brought me to OLCV 25 years ago. OLCV shares my values and puts them into effective political action. OLCV has earned my respect and financial support."
The work that we do every day matters. For over 50 years we have been a catalyst of holding our state elected officials accountable for how they voted on climate, providing easily accessible resources to assist voters in making informed decisions, and advocating for monumental climate legislation.
Give now [https://secure.ngpvan.com/5zGcLKjrckS5lWLLpIGsGA2?am=20&amtOpts=&recurringAm=20&recurringAmtOpts=&is_optimized_ask=true&contactdata=pZHxQXbdScqlk6TlSMzwITtDBcuMUn/ikoYwX8PDNzxly/U/scE30CxVhn2Q6QEcP8qvV443Xk+Tl3+iPh6GN2Azm6bL9CNL922vsoan36km4W/rJltyvFRVqgTthP0N]
As long as we have the support of our members, there is nothing we can’t achieve in the new year. Will you join Pete and I in the new year?
Please give a generous gift of $50 now so OLCV can continue to march on in this fight in 2026. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/t28-fgcXRUmjEwNoP96SgQ2]
Thanks for your support,
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/AV/AVLCV/1/70877/images/April%27s%20Signature.png]
April Christenson (she/her)
OLCV Development Director
P.S.: We are in the big push for end of year fundraising. If you would no longer like to receive emails related to end of year fundraising, please fill out this form here to opt-out [https://secure.everyaction.com/c2_U_ki-NEyU8Gdkttquug2] but still remain on our general email list!
https://www.facebook.com/oregonlcv [https://www.facebook.com/oregonlcv] https://bsky.app/profile/olcv.org [https://bsky.app/profile/olcv.org] https://www.instagram.com/olcv/?next=%2F [https://www.instagram.com/olcv/?next=%2F] https://x.com/OLCV [https://x.com/OLCV]

Oregon League of Conservation Voters
1110 SE Alder Suite 220
Portland, OR 97214
United States

name: The Antimonopolist
subject: Books! Books! Books!
body:
Hi friends, I'll be at Chatham Bookstore in Columbia County this Saturday talking about Joe Costello's terrific new book on the power, technology, and democracy in the 21st century. If you're in the area, I'd love to see you--sign up here. I also wanted to share my new essay in the New York Review of Books, which dives into multilevel marketing, Brigid Read's amazing new book, Little Bosses Everywhere about multi-level marketing (its more important and pervasive than you think- and more exploi

| | | | [](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/7e7755ac?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21)
---
[The Antimonopolist](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/13d8fe45?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21)
| [Books! Books! Books!](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/0698472e?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21)
---
| By Zephyr Teachout • 20 Nov 2025 | [View in browser](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/cd403dcd?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21)
---|---
[View in browser](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/9a3aa859?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21)
Just think how much wisdom in one aisle of one old bookstore alone. (Photo by cometstarmoon from Madison, Wisconsin.)
| | | Hi friends,I'll be at Chatham Bookstore in Columbia County this Saturday talking about Joe Costello's [terrific new book](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/e40c0fd9?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21) on the power, technology, and democracy in the 21st century. If you're in the area, I'd love to see you--sign up [here. ](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/cf2fe0e3?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21)I also wanted to share my new essay in the New York Review of Books, which dives into multilevel marketing, Brigid Read's amazing new book, [_Little Bosses Everywhere_](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/9071611d?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21) __ about multi-level marketing (its more important and pervasive than you think- and more exploitative), and the deep historical exploration of consumer collectives in Kathleen Thelen's new book, [_Attention Shoppers_](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/00f286af?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21).**Please**[**read the whole thing here**](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/5c5f21d7?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21)**. An excerpt:**
> When people join an MLM, they take on an oddly split economic identity. Associates certainly see themselves as sellers, but in the data that (Bridget) Read shares they usually look like exploited buyers. Put another way, they seem to be exploited both as consumers (they were lied to about a product) and as employees (they can't make money from their labor). They are at once the company's target audience and the worker, the muscle.
> This figure--the worker-consumer--doesn't fit neatly in the postindustrial division of American economic activity. Naturally, workers are almost always also consumers, and vice versa--but our economic system relies on a clear distinction between the two roles, each of which has its own associated legal categories, moral values, and organizing principles. Labor law was designed to support the wage earner who sells her time to industrial owners, protecting her right to organize and ensuring basic humane treatment. Consumer protection law was developed to shield the buyer who uses his wages to purchase goods, safeguarding him from deceptive advertising and dangerous products.
> Separating people into these two roles meant deemphasizing their power as citizens who might exercise some measure of economic agency by shaping the terms of exchange itself. This cordoning off of workers and consumers fed into a more general midcentury trend toward the depoliticization of economic life and the conflation of freedom with consumer "choice."
And one more strong recommendation: this weekend, I read Ayad Akhtar's extraordinary 2020 book,__[_Homeland Elegies_](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/3f5aa2ce?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21). The sentences are emotional and intellectual whirlwinds, the characters moving and iconic, and the central premise, a not-non-fiction response to a demand for confession, is gorgeous in its insistence on literature as having its own independent power. So, a great book. Imagine my excitement then to find that Chapter 8 has a character in diatribe against Bork and talking about how the destruction of antitrust laws was related to civil rights:
> If you were black in the '80s, Mike said, you couldn't ignore what the new laws really mean. Black banks, black insurers were getting bought up white-owned holding companies and turning their back on their new black customer; these growing conglomerates were not locally-owned, had no local stakes.... and connected to all this not very-thinly-veiled commercial racism was something people couldn't forget; that their civil rights battles had owned more to black-owned businesses than most would ever understand. Economic independence was essential to the battle for full rights, the money to sustain the struggle had to come from somewhere; most often, it came from local blacks businesses. Black grocery store owners bankrolled bus boycotts; black drugstore owners financed "wade-ins" at segregated beaches; black funeral owners pulled their money from white banks until WHITES ONLY sings were removed from water fountains and bathrooms.
There's a lot more, throughout this chapter and fantastic [book](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/d6a784b0?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21), but definitely worth reading. None of this makes up for the devastatingly bad decision in the Meta case, or Trump's effort with Mark Andreessen to block all state and local AI tech regulation, but reading is more than its own reward, I hope. More soon,
Zephyr[support this newsletter](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/666c09f5?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21)
---

clean_body: Hi friends, I'll be at Chatham Bookstore in Columbia County this Saturday talking about Joe Costello's terrific new book on the power, technology, and democracy in the 21st century. If you're in the area, I'd love to see you--sign up here. I also wanted to share my new essay in the New York Review of Books, which dives into multilevel marketing, Brigid Read's amazing new book, Little Bosses Everywhere about multi-level marketing (its more important and pervasive than you think- and more exploi | | | | | | By Zephyr Teachout • 20 Nov 2025 | ---|--- Just think how much wisdom in one aisle of one old bookstore alone. (Photo by cometstarmoon from Madison, Wisconsin.) | | | Hi friends,I'll be at Chatham Bookstore in Columbia County this Saturday talking about Joe Costello's on the power, technology, and democracy in the 21st century. If you're in the area, I'd love to see you--sign up also wanted to share my new essay in the New York Review of Books, which dives into multilevel marketing, Brigid Read's amazing new book, about multi-level marketing (its more important and pervasive than you think- and more exploitative), and the deep historical exploration of consumer collectives in Kathleen Thelen's new book, . An excerpt: When people join an MLM, they take on an oddly split economic identity. Associates certainly see themselves as sellers, but in the data that (Bridget) Read shares they usually look like exploited buyers. Put another way, they seem to be exploited both as consumers (they were lied to about a product) and as employees (they can't make money from their labor). They are at once the company's target audience and the worker, the muscle. This figure--the worker-consumer--doesn't fit neatly in the postindustrial division of American economic activity. Naturally, workers are almost always also consumers, and vice versa--but our economic system relies on a clear distinction between the two roles, each of which has its own associated legal categories, moral values, and organizing principles. Labor law was designed to support the wage earner who sells her time to industrial owners, protecting her right to organize and ensuring basic humane treatment. Consumer protection law was developed to shield the buyer who uses his wages to purchase goods, safeguarding him from deceptive advertising and dangerous products. Separating people into these two roles meant deemphasizing their power as citizens who might exercise some measure of economic agency by shaping the terms of exchange itself. This cordoning off of workers and consumers fed into a more general midcentury trend toward the depoliticization of economic life and the conflation of freedom with consumer "choice." And one more strong recommendation: this weekend, I read Ayad Akhtar's extraordinary 2020 book,. The sentences are emotional and intellectual whirlwinds, the characters moving and iconic, and the central premise, a not-non-fiction response to a demand for confession, is gorgeous in its insistence on literature as having its own independent power. So, a great book. Imagine my excitement then to find that Chapter 8 has a character in diatribe against Bork and talking about how the destruction of antitrust laws was related to civil rights: If you were black in the '80s, Mike said, you couldn't ignore what the new laws really mean. Black banks, black insurers were getting bought up white-owned holding companies and turning their back on their new black customer; these growing conglomerates were not locally-owned, had no local stakes.... and connected to all this not very-thinly-veiled commercial racism was something people couldn't forget; that their civil rights battles had owned more to black-owned businesses than most would ever understand. Economic independence was essential to the battle for full rights, the money to sustain the struggle had to come from somewhere; most often, it came from local blacks businesses. Black grocery store owners bankrolled bus boycotts; black drugstore owners financed "wade-ins" at segregated beaches; black funeral owners pulled their money from white banks until WHITES ONLY sings were removed from water fountains and bathrooms. There's a lot more, throughout this chapter and fantastic , but definitely worth reading. None of this makes up for the devastatingly bad decision in the Meta case, or Trump's effort with Mark Andreessen to block all state and local AI tech regulation, but reading is more than its own reward, I hope. More soon, Zephyr | | The Antimonopolist (C) 2025 -

name: Tom Fitton
subject: Why I’m thankful for you
body:
As Thanksgiving approaches, I want to express my gratitude for your support and the trust you place in our team here at Judicial Watch.

| [ | |
---
|
--- ](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=77853&L=30946&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9uYXRlL3RoYW5rLXlvdS0yMTYvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPU1heSZ1dG1fdGVybT1Ob24tRG9ub3Jz&hash=aafc90fdace25a5a1871d30fa9a66413e29290f69056683c8a490d5fbaf2a4d2) |
---
| Dear Fellow American,

As Thanksgiving approaches, I want to express my gratitude for your engagement with Judicial Watch. Our supporters undergird all our work fighting **_for_** the rule of law and **_against_** public corruption.
Especially those who make financial gifts. You see, almost all our funding comes from generous citizens just like you.
We could not undertake **_any_** of our investigations or lawsuits without the wonderful commitments our donors make to Judicial Watch.
On top of how you've expanded our reach nationwide by not only reading our content, but sharing it with your friends and family, _[I'd like to invite you to consider joining Judicial Watch as a financial supporter too today](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=77853&L=30946&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9uYXRlL3RoYW5rLXlvdS0yMTYvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPU1heSZ1dG1fdGVybT1Ob24tRG9ub3Jz&hash=aafc90fdace25a5a1871d30fa9a66413e29290f69056683c8a490d5fbaf2a4d2)_.
| [ JOIN JUDICIAL WATCH TODAY ](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=77853&L=30946&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9uYXRlL3RoYW5rLXlvdS0yMTYvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPU1heSZ1dG1fdGVybT1Ob24tRG9ub3Jz&hash=aafc90fdace25a5a1871d30fa9a66413e29290f69056683c8a490d5fbaf2a4d2)
---

Our members' gifts have built the most powerful conservative litigation engine in the country.

And they've made 2025 one of the most impactful years ever for Judicial Watch.

**_Thanks to our members_****... **

**Judicial Watch fought for the sanctity of Election Day at the Supreme Court in October** and we have just been granted a second landmark case to be heard before the High Court to prevent the unlawful counting of ballots received after Election Day in Mississippi!

**_Thanks to our members_****... **

**We passed the milestone of removing over 5 MILLION INELIGIBLE NAMES from voter rolls through successful Judicial Watch lawsuits** and we're currently in Federal Court in Oregon, California, and Illinois to clean up the voter rolls in those states (because dirty voter rolls are open invitations to fraud).

**_Thanks to our members_****... **

**Our Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuits continue to expose the Clinton/Obama/Biden Deep State efforts targeting President Trump** over the last decade. _The recent indictment of James Comey_ proves these corrupt actors who led the coup against Trump are being held accountable (while names fade from the headlines, Judicial Watch never stops fighting to bring wrongdoers to justice).

**_And thanks to our members_****... **

**Friends and foes alike continue to regard Judicial Watch as America's most effective public records litigator** in forcing government transparency and holding corrupt political operatives accountable to the American People.

You can see we've had a great year. And your engagement with our message is part of that. **Thank you**.

But as a watchdog organization, Judicial Watch's work is never done.

My team is already preparing for an onslaught of challenges to election integrity in 2026 from Soros-funded groups and radical Leftist activists.

**And it 's only through the financial support of Judicial Watch donors that we have a chance to defeat their schemes**.

_[Will you join their ranks today by making a first-time financial gift to Judicial Watch](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=77853&L=30946&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9uYXRlL3RoYW5rLXlvdS0yMTYvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPU1heSZ1dG1fdGVybT1Ob24tRG9ub3Jz&hash=aafc90fdace25a5a1871d30fa9a66413e29290f69056683c8a490d5fbaf2a4d2)_?

[ YOUR GIFT WILL MAKE MORE VICTORIES POSSIBLE ](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=77853&L=30946&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9uYXRlL3RoYW5rLXlvdS0yMTYvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPU1heSZ1dG1fdGVybT1Ob24tRG9ub3Jz&hash=aafc90fdace25a5a1871d30fa9a66413e29290f69056683c8a490d5fbaf2a4d2)
---

Every contribution counts and is deeply appreciated.

_THANK YOU for supporting Judicial Watch by staying up to date on our work_.

**And THANK YOU for considering a gift!**

Sincerely,

Thomas Fitton
President

name: Tony Thurmond
subject: Another billionaire in the race
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tony Thurmond for Governor 2026, FPPC# 1461509
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/thurmondforgovernor [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/thurmondforgovernor]
Tony here.
You probably saw the news, another billionaire just jumped into the Governor’s race. And let’s be honest -- this is the choice in front of us.
California can follow a path shaped by billionaires... or we can build a future shaped by working people.
I’m running because I know what families in this state are going through - the cost of housing, the strain on schools, the fight for fair wages and worker protections. I’ve spent my life standing with unions and working families, not the billionaire class.
And while billionaires can fund their campaigns with one check, our campaign is powered by grassroots supporters across California. That’s our strength - and it’s how we win.
I’m fighting for:
- More good-paying, middle-class jobs
- Real investments in schools and childcare
- More affordable housing
- Single-payer healthcare
- Stronger rights for workers and unions
This is exactly the agenda the billionaire class doesn’t want - and exactly why we must organize now.
If you can, chip in today to show that California isn’t for sale. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/thurmondforgovernor]
If you want to get even more involved, I’d love to see you at our upcoming holiday party:
Wednesday, December 3, 2025
6:00 PM – 7:30 PM
Brix Brewery - 2400 Mandela Parkway, Oakland
We’ll share good food, great Brix beverages, and time with supporters who make this movement possible.
RSVP or donate by clicking here. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/TonyDec3]
Hope to see you there - and thank you for being part of this fight.
Sincerely,
Tony

name: Nina Ahmad
subject: Two Important Updates - Exciting Moves for Philadelphia!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nina for Philly
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/nina2025 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/nina2025]
Hi Friend,
I hope this email finds you healthy and enjoying this beautiful fall! My year-to-date as your City Councilmember has been busy and productive. It’s been an honor to hear so many of your ideas, concerns, and hopes as I’ve traveled all across Philadelphia.
I want to share two important updates on my work that took place this week and are particularly meaningful to me.
Today, we celebrated the passage of my bill - The Menstrual and Menopause Protections Ordinance . This legislation amends Philadelphia’s Fair Practices Ordinance to ensure that employees experiencing menstruation, perimenopause, or menopause are protected from discrimination at work and are entitled to reasonable accommodations—just as with pregnancy or childbirth.
I am thrilled to pass this innovative legislation that works to break the stigma around menstruation, perimenopause, and menopause - and ensures fair, practical workplace protections for all. This effectively closes a gap in protections and represents a real step forward toward workplace equity.
On Tuesday, I convened a group of experts, advocates, and survivors of human trafficking to launch a citywide effort focused on human trafficking awareness and prevention. I am committed to this ongoing effort to build a safer, more informed Philadelphia - one that refuses to leave survivors unheard and unseen . I am deeply grateful to our partners and to my colleagues for their support. Ironically, my convening coincided with our House and Senate Democrats voting unanimously to demand the release of the Epstein Files by our government. I know that Americans are united in this call for accountability, prevention, and justice.
Ahead of 2026's international focus on Philadelphia, we will be continuing to work on this comprehensive, multilingual awareness campaign to build citywide awareness and foster a culture of vigilance and prevention human trafficking across Philadelphia. We are calling on every resident, neighborhood, and visitor to stand together against exploitation. Please stay tuned for much more to come on this important ongoing project and click below to read more about this important work. [https://easternstates.heart.org/2025/06/18/philadelphia-city-council-approves-500000-to-improve-access-to-healthy-foods/]
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/nina2025 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/nina2025]
https://www.nbcphiladelphia.com/news/local/philadelphia-first-ever-human-trafficking-awareness-and-prevention-campaign/4303890/?_branch_match_id=1032709934466964794&utm_medium=share&_branch_referrer=H4sIAAAAAAAAA8soKSkottLXz0tKLsjIzElMSc0B0ol6iQUFejmZedn6HiYFWSWlhcb5kUn2dUWpaalFRZl56fFJRfnlxalFts4ZRfm5qQC9qn4bRgAAAA%3D%3D [https://www.nbcphiladelphia.com/news/local/philadelphia-first-ever-human-trafficking-awareness-and-prevention-campaign/4303890/?_branch_match_id=1032709934466964794&utm_medium=share&_branch_referrer=H4sIAAAAAAAAA8soKSkottLXz0tKLsjIzElMSc0B0ol6iQUFejmZedn6HiYFWSWlhcb5kUn2dUWpaalFRZl56fFJRfnlxalFts4ZRfm5qQC9qn4bRgAAAA%3D%3D]
https://www.audacy.com/kywnewsradio/news/local/philly-anti-human-trafficking-initiative [https://www.audacy.com/kywnewsradio/news/local/philly-anti-human-trafficking-initiative]
It’s so important to me that I continue to hear your thoughts and priorities . I would love it if you could respond to this email to let me know how you think we are doing and where we should be headed. I need eyes and ears across the City and beyond in order to do my best work.
As always, I am endlessly optimistic for Philadelphia and deeply grateful for your support. I carry you with me each and every day.
I look forward to hearing more from you and ask for your continued partnership in the hard work of improving the lives of every Philadelphian. I know that we can do this together!
Please also consider donating to my campaign to ensure my ability to keep fighting for Philadelphia! [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/nina2025]
Thank you again,
Councilmember Nina Ahmad
Donate [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/nina2025]
Please donate $25, $50, $100, $1000 or more to help me continue protecting our fundamental rights, ensuring every eligible voter has their voice heard, and investing in critical programs. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/nina2025]

Ahead of 2026's international focus on Philadelphia, we will be continuing to work on this comprehensive, multilingual awareness campaign to build citywide awareness and foster a culture of vigilance and prevention human trafficking across Philadelphia. We are calling on every resident, neighborhood, and visitor to stand together against exploitation. Please stay tuned for much more to come on this important ongoing project and click below to read more about this important work. [] [] [] [] It’s so important to me that I continue to hear your thoughts and priorities . I would love it if you could respond to this email to let me know how you think we are doing and where we should be headed. I need eyes and ears across the City and beyond in order to do my best work. As always, I am endlessly optimistic for Philadelphia and deeply grateful for your support. I carry you with me each and every day. I look forward to hearing more from you and ask for your continued partnership in the hard work of improving the lives of every Philadelphian. I know that we can do this together! Please also consider donating to my campaign to ensure my ability to keep fighting for Philadelphia! [] Thank you again, Councilmember Nina Ahmad Donate [] Please donate $25, $50, $100, $1000 or more to help me continue protecting our fundamental rights, ensuring every eligible voter has their voice heard, and investing in critical programs. [] [] [] [] [] Paid for by Nina for Philly Nina for Philly PO BOX 27339 Philadelphia, PA 19118 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: .

name: Kamala Harris
subject: Tennessee needs us
body:
We have an important special election in Tennessee in less than two weeks. Polls are already open, and Democrats urgently need our support to win.
If that is all you need to hear, please send a contribution to Tennessee Democrats and my work using this link:
[**https://act.kamalaharris.com/go/Tennessee-Democrats**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-ftp-tndems?refcode=em251120-tennessee&refcode2=795_2563979_8Fn8-O&akid=795%2E2563979%2E8Fn8-O)
I am going to speak truth right here:
I have been in elected office long enough to know that leaders in Washington look to two groups for feedback: who is writing the checks and who is going to vote.
So let’s make sure they understand that we donate and we vote — so they need to listen to us.
Right now, there are some powerful forces that are trying to suggest that folks are alone and without power. Powerful forces are trying to divide us and to tell us that we and our needs do not matter.
We need to let them hear our voice and demand they be responsive to the people. **This is a moment where we reclaim: This is _our_ country.**
One of the best ways we can be clear about that is by supporting Tennessee Democrats in this special election. Right here. Right now.
I was on the ground in Tennessee this week, and I am doing everything I can. But Tennessee Democrats need _your_ help to win. With less than two weeks until Election Day and early voting underway, the time to act is now. **So if reclaiming our country is meaningful to you, I am asking you to donate today.**
[**Please make a contribution to support my work and the Tennessee Democratic Party. With polls already open, we need everyone who cares about this race and the future of our country to step up now. This is our moment to show that the power is with the people.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-ftp-tndems?refcode=em251120-tennessee&refcode2=795_2563979_8Fn8-O&akid=795%2E2563979%2E8Fn8-O)

name: Brianna Titone
subject: Remembering the lives that were lost
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Brianna for Treasurer
body:
Today is Trans Day of Remembrance, a day for us to remember and honor the transgender people whose lives were taken by hate and violence. | | | [](http://links.briannaforco.com/s/c/IEj4DOxH3Oj4G-iLPFdRibt_SApyiRyU2-4n7mfyLP-tPraUEKc0J8VhnPhCY6Oz-1ucUvHZQxLdYYLumr4gfYBoiGfYgyDVIA1TX1Ota8l2ASPTgMmOnUMgfTpRMnQk7hGpz-Vva0Ntj2XQSG-ntkSDQPe82If1uNQwgM4wAK3HGceQPE4xyA8bNggZUp8AF7YdiO-ton3pRa9nQcAsp7g5Zb7GSHPTbrLY_4GDp6k7YmCsuDDWcEHJL9fKNoYHgTlK-1hiq0lCKtK4Jsxu2bkjSFexqPhmjFN65tokHjf1DhF-T64uPZKDH87JrALPuubYsV5qlx4XlWLnnd1oBpRqqySfGoOHqr21NDeuHhvvKROfVSilEMzOhHv86Y255mm_mwD9AUoxOlCv1Ffk9lklgoO4iwpD1r10zjSSs7e78UG4tE2nNm57RMhNGGkmAm2KPyAwruY3aMTETKsEsHV7jpUu5ZN7d_bMyenGQFFnOooUpwE13g0iQZAgyEjT0OOZfvzt4jHk1dvO74eHUcIcRtlCKg5mhGbTPNMnGJ15JQ0jhghBge46Qy-SNHmq2NrkoGNqa24udZYv5Jc1HY5kg6umM7PogGKOCFvF1ffT1u5B0zuBzd9o9i_aXuy8PY4Vt0TxJ8DbEXlmrTjwvZpI3vHXiuhclv3aQvJbbGVcRkoDRPmj4XkCH8zXgZmgiEaTGe0sg8yCaUgLxeyQP4kyGUHB610YajTG2WL_hh-GeRz8EFCF2itVBh-LTXtUqAMDw349Q21D0C7Lj5Hyy-3yxNFCJ_zb5umRGmyQUNqWq_y3k4JottvgyhjG1rZ5LgXgTizW2bJ5WkZapEXqmuczc3nI7Eg8jO609rfqWKnrCS_kppbpFcjFStuvq2_kzE-JBw98UDO1B7uAEAwI7ttVtAghwUjacj9NGUx-1cpzdhyh-7q3bHBx/Hl2aREaHbdm03Tr_k0dC_WrzyqLj-dsv/15) Peter, Today is **Trans Day of Remembrance** , a day for us to remember and honor the transgender people whose lives were taken by hate and violence. Today, our community carries the names and stories of people who should still be here. It’s also a reminder that our community has survived by taking care of one another, generation after generation. Remembering those we've lost to anti-trans violence isn’t the end. We have to keep fighting for the living. Here in Colorado, we remember the victims whose lives were lost as a result of the anti–LBGTQ–motivated mass shooting in 2022 at Club Q. [](http://links.briannaforco.com/s/c/uYpzjqT3cQRdPgAvhNgrMyLcg7seEKZ7rmEVDBoXR7xjD_kjYqsWuIiZzZY0vs0oqftRGxIYJUeMMPJE6biOoDFM-n51Ttg2OlgzF8kkkRkiVMTyeI_0b2UwkbzQmbtjVbjKuUk5OIPkMeM3eGqu0eK_SGFcDXwZqoT-9bRZDA50vQ0F0S_xSnqZ5bX0GI0d_JbN75NZoM3R9I2jdDgEkH2snW4kHqzf_LtPw4M67kOCBfH1UlF4plyBTCnaGMXdv8w02kB0UI9sRaSdUrN2lVk5MncSyfH8J6Tvvi9eRqwu0wNTFSvexNGkhAzxxGrO2beKf-v3CNPSr-V_XW1iSViMouDpXuNZuxQdvM--40WLxi5J1qiylQcUdqvJFdSd7iY896bhflw0zPncnac0Z1RcevIiKCkAIH1ViYcayVvkI9ztlY3HW-AkGLGk7HPTgwGsEQ47n51ruHBXHpeEVFyo8DxrsV2GhfQ72gw-Tq51mLPxs1KJQe1bVvNSG3-oD5oFRWkHqOq9KgpAT5kEm9i5lAi11BfqxUs4GdqJKu83O4A4zLHLf_Nxw2W2Edj47TlNqbDXI4TzaX-59Znf4xZhi8nQxSkwzvQS1L4XX_1HP2_ZyOziQCa-2UJMQphf9VIb807ipnyCl9TrpphKOYvTkAfT-BNl5n5fNqlzQ9mz6UIodMUq4-nSUjJ2JtmXlWUbOi8MuWrNagbiA93y9-0MMFRR3FOqKyk4aLP5beTC4LYgOeHzeaVvDvgiILmhDwO--mTUr47sdrIbRkJfQyRoG60kgsbgmARY-6aY9ehGQUtUDjgNS1OgaEnRs_r6hCR-OLe4YEMkJYAL6brf8tFAAikNJ21j-F0NqLHHpM3-8slOXrKY_lktRokBOoM3-QBrEpvOzoTdmeiMtA8ow31EabZ1tHfPUDXjdghLUeJ5UzF2oP_sscxE/_1lyb0IRRTEhwIcFbSN0SCpBRrvFIbRG/15) This is a deeply personal day for me. As a transgender woman, I know the fear that comes from living every day as my true self. But I also know how powerful it is to see others like me take the brave step to embrace who they are. No one should have to fear for their lives because of who they are. We must stand together to protect our transgender community, stand up to hate, and create a future where everyone is seen, valued, and safe. In honor of the lives that have been taken out of hate, we must work towards a safer and compassionate future. – Brianna

* * *

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family.
